edges.evidence_quote: A complex dispute involving disgruntled subordinates and territory claims by New York 's Genovese crime family led to Bruno's murder in 1980.
edges.context: The Genovese family claimed territory and was involved in the dispute leading to Bruno's murder.

edges.evidence_quote: Bruno used his contacts and his own business mind to maintain respect and power among other Mafia bosses in the country. He expanded the family's profit and operations in Atlantic City, which, due in part to its location within the Philadelphia metro area, had naturally become known as the Philadelphia family's turf.
edges.context: Angelo Bruno was a close ally of Carlo Gambino, solidifying his position as boss.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1970, Samuel "Beyah" Christian and other African-American organized crime figures formed the Black Mafia to take control of illegal activities in the Black neighborhoods of Philadelphia from the Italian Mafia, a venture which was partially successful.
edges.context: The Black Mafia formed to take control from the Philadelphia family, leading to conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: The crime reporter George Anastasia has described the organization as one of the two most dysfunctional Mafia families in the United States, along with the Patriarca crime family of New England.
edges.context: Both families are described as the most dysfunctional, but no direct relationship is stated.

edges.evidence_quote: A complex dispute involving disgruntled subordinates and territory claims by New York's Genovese crime family led to Bruno's murder in 1980.
edges.context: Genovese family had territory claims against Philadelphia family, leading to Bruno's murder.

edges.evidence_quote: Although the Greek mob had long been a close ally and partner of the Philadelphia Mafia...
edges.context: Greek mob was a close ally and partner of Philadelphia Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: When Greek-American gangster Chelsais "Steve" Bouras, boss of the Philadelphia Greek Mob, began intruding on the methamphetamine trade in Philadelphia and refused to pay Scarfo's street tax, Scarfo had him killed.
edges.context: Scarfo had Bouras killed for refusing to pay street tax, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Giovanni "John" Stanfa, a Sicilian-born mobster with the support and endorsement of the influential Gambino and Genovese families in New York, was named boss of the Philadelphia family in 1990.
edges.context: Gambino family supported Stanfa's installation as boss of Philadelphia family.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1995, Louis Turra, leader of a Philadelphia drug gang, the 10th & Oregon Crew, was severely beaten by Merlino's men, allegedly for failing to pay a Mafia street tax on the gang's illegal earnings.
edges.context: Merlino's men beat Turra for failing to pay street tax, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Merlino was friends with Steve "Gorilla" Mondevergine, president of the Philadelphia chapter of the Pagan's Motorcycle Club. Merlino sometimes used the Pagans to help settle underworld disputes.
edges.context: Merlino used Pagans to settle disputes, indicating cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: The alliance between the Philadelphia Mafia and the Pagans prevented the Pagans' rivals, the Hells Angels, from expanding into Pagan territory in southeast Pennsylvania and New Jersey.
edges.context: Pagans and Hells Angels are rivals, with Pagans preventing Hells Angels expansion.

edges.evidence_quote: During the 1990s, Merlino was also aligned with members of the Junior Black Mafia.
edges.context: Merlino aligned with Junior Black Mafia members.

edges.evidence_quote: A complex dispute involving disgruntled subordinates and territory claims by New York's Genovese crime family led to Bruno's murder in 1980.
edges.context: The Genovese family's territory claims contributed to the murder of Philadelphia boss Angelo Bruno, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Since his release from prison, Borgesi has traveled to Rhode Island forming an alliance with Patriarca crime family underboss Matthew Guglielmetti.
edges.context: Philadelphia boss George Borgesi formed an alliance with the Patriarca crime family's underboss.

edges.evidence_quote: A complex dispute involving disgruntled subordinates and territory claims by New York's Genovese crime family led to Bruno's murder in 1980.
edges.context: The Genovese family's territory claims contributed to the murder of Philadelphia boss Angelo Bruno.

edges.evidence_quote: Martines joined the Philadelphia family in 1990 and became a key member of John Stanfa's faction which feuded with the 'Young Turks' led by Joey Merlino during the Third Philadelphia Mafia War of the early 1990s.
edges.context: The Stanfa faction and Merlino faction were in a violent feud during the Third Philadelphia Mafia War.

edges.evidence_quote: A complex dispute involving disgruntled subordinates and territory claims by New York's Genovese crime family led to Bruno's murder in 1980.
edges.context: Genovese family had territory claims that contributed to the murder of Philadelphia boss Angelo Bruno.

edges.target: North Jersey faction of the Bruno family
edges.evidence_quote: Bocchino was initially a member of the North Jersey faction of the Bruno family and was in the Newark crew headed by Antonio Caponigro during the 1960s and 1970s.
edges.context: The North Jersey faction was a part of the Bruno family (Philadelphia crime family).

edges.evidence_quote: Along with Joey Merlino, he was a leading member of the "Young Turks" faction which challenged the leadership of family boss John Stanfa during the early 1990s.
edges.context: The Young Turks were a faction within the Philadelphia crime family that challenged the boss.

edges.evidence_quote: After the Young Turks killed Stanfa loyalist Felix Bocchino in January 1992, Ciancaglini survived a retaliatory shotgun attack outside his home in South Philadelphia on March 3, 1992.
edges.context: The Young Turks and the Stanfa faction were in conflict during the Third Philadelphia Mafia War.

edges.evidence_quote: A complex dispute involving disgruntled subordinates and territory claims by New York's Genovese crime family led to Bruno's murder in 1980.
edges.context: The Genovese family had territory claims against the Philadelphia family, leading to conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: He testified against Genovese crime family boss Vincent Gigante in 1997 and claimed Gigante ordered 6 murder contracts as retaliation for murdering Philadelphia bosses Angelo Bruno and Phil Testa without approval from the other crime families, specifically in New York.
edges.context: Genovese boss Gigante ordered murder contracts against Philadelphia family members as retaliation.

edges.evidence_quote: He was scheduled to testify against Gambino crime family boss John Gotti on January 21, 1992, however Gambino underboss Sammy "the Bull" Gravano agreed to testify instead.
edges.context: Philadelphia family member Leonetti was to testify against Gambino boss Gotti, indicating legal conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Originally an associate of the Patriarca crime family, he joined the Philadelphia mob and became inducted in 1998.
edges.context: Robert Luisi Jr. moved from Patriarca family to Philadelphia family, showing a transfer of membership.

edges.evidence_quote: Jackie DiNorscio ... later defects to the Lucchese crime family due to a bloody internal feud within the Philadelphia crime family.
edges.context: A Philadelphia soldier defected to the Lucchese family, indicating a structural tie.

edges.evidence_quote: The Philadelphia Crime Family (also known as the Buscetta Crime Family) are led by ruthless mob boss Angelo "Angie" Buscetta and are considered enemies of the DiMeo/Soprano crime family.
edges.context: In the video game, the Philadelphia family is an enemy of the DiMeo/Soprano family.

edges.evidence_quote: Angelo Bruno ... and his consigliere ... are sent to London by Meyer Lansky (a powerful Jewish American mafia boss and one of the heads of the National Crime Syndicate) to negotiate a deal with the Kray twins.
edges.context: Philadelphia boss Bruno acted on behalf of the National Crime Syndicate in negotiations.
